name: Valle de Iruelas and Puerto de Casillas Loop
name_original: Charco del cura, Valle de Iruelas, Puerto de Casillas y El Castañar – circular desde El Tiemblo
description: This difficult 27.9-mile mountain bike loop from El Tiemblo explores Valle de Iruelas, Puerto de Casillas, and El Castañar.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around El Tiemblo traverse a diverse landscape characterized by rolling hills, dense pine and oak forests, and the significant presence of reservoirs like El Burguillo and Picadas. The region features varied terrain, including challenging ascents to mountain passes and ridges, as well as scenic routes through valleys and along waterways. This area offers a mix of natural beauty and technical riding opportunities for mountain bikers. Best mountain bike trails around El Tiemblo The most popular mountain bike trail is Valle de Iruelas and Puerto de Casillas Loop, a 27.9 miles (44.9 km) trail that takes about 4 hours 22 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers significant elevation changes through a scenic valley and over a mountain pass. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is El Burguillo Reservoir – Casillas loop from El Tiemblo, a difficult 34.9 miles (56.2 km) path. This route provides extensive views of the reservoir and surrounding hilly terrain. Local mountain bikers also love the San Juan Ridge – Labores Stream Pool loop from Rozas de Puerto Real, a 28.0 miles (45.1 km) trail leading through a prominent ridge and past a stream pool, often completed in about 3 hours 44 minutes. Mountain biking around El Tiemblo is defined by reservoir-side paths, challenging ridge climbs, and forested valleys. The extensive network of routes offers options for various ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in El Tiemblo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 90 reviews. More than 1700 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore El Tiemblo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there in El Tiemblo?
A: El Tiemblo offers a wide selection of mountain bike trails, with over 100 routes available for exploration. These trails traverse diverse landscapes, including rolling hills, dense forests, and scenic reservoir areas.

Q: What is the typical difficulty level of mountain bike trails in El Tiemblo?
A: The mountain bike trails around El Tiemblo primarily cater to intermediate and advanced riders. The majority of routes are classified as moderate (66 trails) or difficult (40 trails), featuring significant elevation changes and technical sections. There are also a few easier options for those looking for a less strenuous ride.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in El Tiemblo?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes in El Tiemblo are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1079431696/valle-de-iruelas-and-puerto-de-casillas-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valle de Iruelas and Puerto de Casillas Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31436082" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Picadas – Picadas Reservoir loop from Rozas de Puerto Real&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the mountain bike trails?
A: You can expect a varied terrain characterized by rolling hills, dense pine and oak forests, and routes alongside significant reservoirs like El Burguillo and Picadas. Trails often include challenging ascents to mountain passes and ridges, as well as flowing sections through valleys and along waterways.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in El Tiemblo?
A: While most trails are moderate to difficult, there are a few easier options available. For families or beginners, it's advisable to look for routes with minimal elevation gain and smoother surfaces. Always check the route details on komoot for specific difficulty ratings and terrain descriptions to ensure it suits your group's ability.

Q: What can I see along the mountain bike trails in El Tiemblo?
A: Many trails offer stunning natural beauty and points of interest. You might pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2788218" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Casillas&lt;/a&gt;, which serves as an entrance to the Valle de Iruelas Nature Reserve, or ride along the shores of the expansive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2788211" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Burguillo Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. The region is also home to the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2788212" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Abuelo Chestnut Tree&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: When is the best time of year to go mountain biking in El Tiemblo?
A: Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for mountain biking in El Tiemblo, with milder temperatures and lush landscapes. Summers can be hot, especially in the middle of the day, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended. Winters are usually mild, but some higher elevation trails might be cooler.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy the most about mountain biking in El Tiemblo?
A: The mountain bike trails in El Tiemblo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 90 reviews. Riders frequently praise the diverse terrain, the challenging climbs, and the beautiful views of the reservoirs and forests. The extensive network of routes also offers something for various ability levels.

Q: Are there any trails that offer views of the reservoirs?
A: Absolutely. Many routes are designed to showcase the region's prominent reservoirs. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17320592" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Burguillo Reservoir – Casillas loop from El Tiemblo&lt;/a&gt; provides extensive views of El Burguillo, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38248738" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Burguillo Reservoir – Burguillo Reservoir loop from Recepción&lt;/a&gt; also offers great reservoir vistas.

Q: Where do the mountain bike trails typically start in El Tiemblo?
A: Many trails originate from or pass through towns and villages like El Tiemblo itself, Rozas de Puerto Real, or near popular points of interest such as the reservoirs. Specific starting points are detailed on each komoot tour page, often with parking information available.

Q: What kind of challenges can advanced mountain bikers expect?
A: Advanced mountain bikers will find plenty of challenging routes with significant elevation gains, technical descents, and long distances. Trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1079431696/valle-de-iruelas-and-puerto-de-casillas-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valle de Iruelas and Puerto de Casillas Loop&lt;/a&gt; offer over 1200 meters of climbing, testing endurance and skill across varied mountain terrain.

Q: Can I find places to eat or drink near the mountain bike trails?
A: While specific cafes directly on remote trails might be limited, you will find various establishments in the towns and villages surrounding the trail network, such as El Tiemblo or Rozas de Puerto Real. These places offer opportunities to refuel before or after your ride.
tips: Located at the foot of the Sierra de Gredos, the Burguillo reservoir is an ideal area to practice all kinds of activities. It offers exceptional views from several points.

The ascent to the port of Casillas is one of the simplest and most essential hiking routes in this part of the Central System.

The port of Casillas is one of the entrances to the Valle de Iruelas Natural Reserve, where if we choose a clear day, we will be able to contemplate a wide landscape, not only a large part of the Natural Reserve with the Burguillo Reservoir in the background over the waters of the Alberche river, but also the Sierras de la Paramera on the horizon and the first foothills of the western Madrid mountains.
Beautiful forest known as El Castañar. An authentic marvel for mountain bike lovers with an infinite number of trails to choose from in good conditions.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The estimated riding time for this challenging mountain bike route is approximately 4 hours and 22 minutes. However, this can vary depending on your fitness level, technical skills, and how often you stop to enjoy the views.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this trail is classified as difficult and requires very good fitness and advanced riding skills. It features significant elevation gains, technical sections, and some parts may even require you to push your bike.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is highly varied, combining stretches of forest tracks (pista forestal) with more technical singletracks (senderos trialeros). You can expect a mix of unpaved, bumpy, and rocky segments, along with numerous 'sube y baja' (up and down) sections throughout the route.

faqs.question: What are the main highlights and viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several stunning features, including the beautiful El Burguillo Reservoir, panoramic views from Puerto de Casillas – Entrance to the Valle de Iruelas Nature Reserve, and the enchanting El Castañar Forest. The route also passes by the Charco del Cura reservoir and offers vistas of the Sierra del Valle.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: Autumn is highly recommended for this trail, especially when the El Castañar Forest transforms with vibrant seasonal colors. The weather is also generally more pleasant for challenging rides during this time.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with sections of other trails such as Valle del Tiétar, Senda del Enebral, Camino de la Artezuela, Camino de Casillas, and Ruta El Pozo de la Nieve PR-AV 21.

faqs.question: Is the Charco del cura, Valle de Iruelas, Puerto de Casillas y El Castañar route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed in the Valle de Iruelas Natural Reserve, it's crucial to keep them on a leash to protect the local wildlife, especially during nesting seasons. Given the trail's difficulty and technical sections, consider if it's suitable for your dog's fitness and paw safety.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for the Valle de Iruelas Natural Reserve?
faqs.answer: Typically, there are no general entrance fees or permits required for hiking or mountain biking within the Valle de Iruelas Natural Reserve. However, it's always a good idea to check the official website of the reserve or local tourism offices for any updated regulations or specific restrictions, especially concerning vehicle access or protected areas.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: This is a circular route starting and ending in El Tiemblo. You can usually find parking in or around the town of El Tiemblo, which serves as a convenient base for accessing the trail.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a mountain bike ride on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's length and difficulty, you should bring plenty of water, energy snacks, a repair kit for your bike, a first-aid kit, and appropriate protective gear. A GPS device with the route loaded is also highly recommended due to the varied terrain and potential for technical sections.

faqs.question: Are there any specific challenging sections I should be aware of?
faqs.answer: Yes, the climb towards Puerto de Casillas is described as a 'long and somewhat tedious climb on a track'. Additionally, the section leading to Portacho del Pozo is noted as the 'most strenuous part of the route', demanding significant effort and advanced riding skills.

waypoints.name: Puerto de Casillas – Entrance to the Valle de Iruelas Nature Reserve
